target_role: Service Technician I - IT Help Desk

rationale.chatgpt: Usable but noticeably below the 9.4 target — reads as a compressed generated draft, not a polished one-page service/help desk resume. Fixing the issues below would move it to ~8.5-8.7; reaching 9+ would require following the v11 anchor style (one page, human summary, compressed strong bullets, grouped skills, no internal grounding labels).

key_strengths: Tight, role-aligned bullets covering ticket handling, SOPs, hardware, and access workflows
Strong sysadmin-relevant tooling surfaced (Linux Photon servers, NUC kiosks, Windows policy, ITSM)
Education section earns its line with a Cum Laude GPA and a coordination/logistics project that maps to ops work
key_weaknesses: Summary contains internal truth-audit phrasing ('access-related troubleshooting... at small-shop scale') that leaked into the final document
Bullet uses title-case keyword label inline: 'Handled Account Creation/Disable Workflows' — looks like a pasted-in tag, not prose
BEAT THE BOMB section dropped two of the strongest presentation anchors from the 9.4 version: the 75% wait-time reduction (10 min to 2.5 min) and the 20-30 staff scope
'Installed and replaced PC hardware components' is flat — no scope or user-facing context

Skills section is one long mixed line with inconsistent capitalization ('Incident response' and 'Technical documentation' lowercase while neighbors are title case); no grouping into Service Desk / Systems / Tools buckets
Layout spills to page 2 carrying only soft skills, military service, and a small project — page 2 feels unintentional rather than earned
specific_fixes: Rewrite summary to remove 'small-shop scale' — replace with role-aligned phrasing about repairs, replacements, SOPs/runbooks, and end-user support in live operational environments
Reword the Account Creation/Disable bullet as prose: 'Supported account creation/disable workflows, access-related troubleshooting, and onboarding support'
Restore the 75% wait-time reduction bullet and a 20-30 staff scope bullet under BEAT THE BOMB
Expand the hardware install bullet to include user-facing context: 'Installed and replaced PC hardware components, supporting repairs and service restoration for user-facing systems'
Group skills into Service Desk & Support / Systems & Hardware / Tools & Documentation buckets with consistent title case
Compress to one page, or make page 2 feel intentional (e.g., dedicated military leadership / projects section with real content density)
tier_definition_match: Matches mid: 'usable but obviously generated.' Does NOT match nearmiss ('almost submit-ready, polish gaps only') — has substantive content gaps and label-leakage issues, not just polish.
